title: Spectrophotometric assay for measuring polyol dehydrogenase activity
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f70om","text":"Polyol dehydrogenases are enzymes that convert polyalcohols into sugars, using NAD+ as hydrogen acceptor. Sorbitol, mannitol or other polyalcohols can be used as substrates, and the activity of these enzymes is assessed by monitoring the production of NADH. This protocol was based on previously published methods (Maret and Auld, 1988; Fernández et al., 1993), and was used to evaluate polyol dehydrogenase activities in the digestive gland of gastropods. With the proper adaptations this procedure can be applied to other dehydrogenases and other organisms.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":82,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"e25us","text":"Prepare the following solutions:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fcgh7","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9mjit","text":"Homogenization medium for marine gastropods","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":43}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7pd4f","text":"(adapted from Stewart et al. 1994 and Moyers et al. 1991)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2pbi9","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fcrt0","text":"Sucrose - 500 mM","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"43ed9","text":"Potassium chloride - 150 mM","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"47sag","text":"Ethylenediamine tetraacetic acid (EDTA), sodium salt - 1 mM","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ai72g","text":"Tris-HCl 50 mM pH 7.4","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a7u6h","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6bddp","text":"This medium, without phenylmethylsulfonyl fluoride (PMSF), can be stored at -20º C in aliquots. To avoid the inactivation of PMSF in aqueous solution, add 5 µl of 200 mM PMSF (in absolute ethanol) per ml of medium just before use (final PMSF concentration 1 mM).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":133,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"u6ug","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d9j7l","text":"Good results were also obtained with freshwater and terrestrial gastropods using this high osmolarity medium.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6q786","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"55p0k","text":"Substrate solutions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6piok","text":"A. 50 mM NAD+solution in water (can be stored frozen in aliquots)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":12,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eubs4","text":"B. 57 mM polyalcohol (sorbitol, mannitol or other) in 50 mM glycine/NaOH buffer pH 10.0","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Homogenize approximately 0.05-0.06 g of digestive gland tissue per ml of cold homogenization medium using a Potter-Elvehjem homogenizer at 1,000 rpm, keeping the tube in ice. Register the exact weight of digestive gland tissue used and the final homogenate volume. Sonicate the homogenate during 3 x 15 s using a bath sonicator, keeping the tube in an ice-water bath. Centrifuge the homogenate at 1,000 g for 5 min. at 4º C, and use the supernatant to assess polyol dehydrogenase activities. Keep samples on ice and use immediately after preparation.

In a tube, mix 100 µl of substrate solution A with 880 µl of substrate solution B and 20 µl of sample, agitate with a vortex and pour into the spectrophotometer. Final concentrations in the assay: 5 mM NAD + , 50 mM polyalcohol. Sample dilution factor in the assay: 50. Measure the absorbance at 340 nm, at 25º C, during 3-5 min (or longer for samples with very low enzyme activity). Make sure that activity is linear in time and proportional to sample concentration. If not, adjust sample dilution. For control, monitor non-specific increases of absorbance at 340 nm using medium without polyalcohol, and subtract if necessary.

Activity (nmol . ml -1 . min -1 ) = Δ Abs340 min -1 x 50* 0.00622 µM -1 NADH ε 340 nm = 6.22 mM -1 . cm -1 * sample dilution factor in the assay Calculate enzyme activity per g of tissue (nmol . g -1 . min -1 ) or per mg of protein (nmol . mg -1 . min -1 ).

Fernández M.R., Jörnvall H., Moreno A., Kaiser R. and Parés X., 1993. Cephalopod alcohol dehydrogenase: purification and enzymatic characterization. FEBS Lett. 328 : 235−238. Maret W. and Auld, D.S., 1988. Purification and characterization of human liver sorbitol dehydrogenase. Biochemistry 27 : 1622−1628. Moyes C.D., Suarez R.K., Brown G.S. and Hochachka P.W., 1991. Peroxisomal β-oxidation: insights from comparative biochemistry. J. Exp. Zool. 260 : 267–273. Stewart J.M., Carlin R.C., Macdonald J.A. and Van Iderstine S., 1994. Fatty acid binding proteins and fatty acid catabolism in marine invertebrates: Peroxisomal β-oxidation. Inv. Rep. Dev. 25 : 73–82.
creator: Alexandre Lobo-Da-Cunha
